section_name: Notice to State; Presentation of Evidence
text:
(a) Unless the petition is dismissed under Section 521.2421 (Petition) (f), the clerk of the court shall send by certified mail to the attorney representing the state a copy of the petition and notice of the hearing if the petitioner’s license was suspended, revoked, or canceled following a conviction for:

(1) an offense under Section 19.05 (Criminally Negligent Homicide) or Sections 49.04-49.08, Penal Code; or

(2) an offense to which Section 521.342 applies.

(b) The court may notify the attorney representing the state of any other hearing on a petition for an occupational driver’s license.

(c) A person who receives notice under Subsection (a) or (b) may attend the hearing and may present evidence at the hearing for or against granting the petition.
markdown:
# Transp. Code
## Title 7
### Chapter 521. Driver's Licenses & Certificates
##### § 521.243 — Notice to State; Presentation of Evidence

(a) Unless the petition is dismissed under Section 521.2421 (Petition) (f), the clerk of the court shall send by certified mail to the attorney representing the state a copy of the petition and notice of the hearing if the petitioner’s license was suspended, revoked, or canceled following a conviction for:

    (1) an offense under Section 19.05 (Criminally Negligent Homicide) or Sections 49.04-49.08, Penal Code; or

    (2) an offense to which Section 521.342 applies.

(b) The court may notify the attorney representing the state of any other hearing on a petition for an occupational driver’s license.

(c) A person who receives notice under Subsection (a) or (b) may attend the hearing and may present evidence at the hearing for or against granting the petition.

*Acts 2023, 88th Leg., R.S., Ch. 386 (H.B.291), Sec. 2, eff. September 1, 2023.*

subsections.label: a
subsections.text: Unless the petition is dismissed under Section 521.2421 (Petition) (f), the clerk of the court shall send by certified mail to the attorney representing the state a copy of the petition and notice of the hearing if the petitioner’s license was suspended, revoked, or canceled following a conviction for:
an offense under Section 19.05 (Criminally Negligent Homicide) or Sections 49.04-49.08, Penal Code; or
an offense to which Section 521.342 applies.

subsections.label: b
subsections.text: The court may notify the attorney representing the state of any other hearing on a petition for an occupational driver’s license.

subsections.label: c
subsections.text: A person who receives notice under Subsection (a) or (b) may attend the hearing and may present evidence at the hearing for or against granting the petition.
